author: derek-larson14
description: Run Claude Code in your sidebar. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Derek Larson
manifest.description: Run Claude Code in your sidebar.
manifest.name: Claude Sidebar

name: Claude Sidebar

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Fixes dead keyboard input on Windows. The stdin parser read OSC sequences until BEL, but Claude Code's startup background-color query gets an ST-terminated reply, so the read loop never exited and swallowed every keystroke. Output kept flowing, so the terminal looked healthy while input was dead from the first keypress. The OSC read now terminates on ST as well, which also lets the CLI receive its color-query answer.

Windows only, present since 1.7.10.

Thanks @bmcd03 for the diagnosis and fix (#93).

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Fixes a crash on startup when a Claude terminal was left open at quit. Restoring the saved terminal leaf mid-layout could tear the view down and abort Obsidian's load (fatal on Linux). Terminal init now waits for the layout to settle.

Thanks @otavio for the diagnosis and fix (#92).

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- #90: Add Ctrl+Shift+C / Ctrl+Shift+V copy/paste on Linux (GNOME Terminal / KDE Konsole convention). Additive and platform-safe — macOS and Windows paths unchanged. Thanks @DZPM.

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description: Fix Windows Ctrl+C copy-out silently failing (#89). Copy now uses the same reliable path as the right-click menu.

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Fix #88: Windows auto-launch now passes per-provider CLI flags (`additionalFlags`), matching macOS/Linux behavior. Thanks @fanjzx for the fix.

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Cross-platform right-click Copy/Paste menu (was Windows-only)
- copyOnSelect — selecting terminal text copies it
- Linux middle-click paste (X11 primary selection)
- Fix macOS Ctrl+click clearing the selection / leaving the terminal stuck mid-drag

Salvaged from PR #71. Thanks @are-scenic and @DZPM.

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Fix #86: click-to-open now works on vault paths with spaces in folder or file names (both backtick-wrapped and plain)

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description: Fix editor Find Next/Previous (and other CodeMirror search) shortcuts breaking while the Claude sidebar was open (#82). The keymap scope is now active only while the terminal has focus, so the editor's own shortcuts work normally when it has focus.

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Feature #85: new Environment variables setting (one KEY=VALUE per line, merged into every session's env). Set CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR here to keep multiple Claude accounts separate.

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Make vault file paths in terminal output clickable.

Claude's terminal output now links any path that resolves to a real file in your vault. Clicking opens or focuses the note in a markdown leaf without disturbing the terminal. Only real files are linked, so there are no dead links.

Thanks to @nexgen for the idea (#83).

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Stop the terminal from swallowing registered Obsidian hotkeys (#82).

The xterm key handler was returning true for keys it did not special-case, so xterm consumed function and find-navigation keys before Obsidian's keymap could run them. When the terminal is focused, keys matching a registered Obsidian hotkey (that the terminal does not need for control codes, typing, or navigation) now propagate to Obsidian.

Note: Find Next / Cmd+F still require an active editor, so they work with a note focused, not from inside the terminal pane itself (no editor there to search).

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
### Fixed
- Resolved a `fit()` ↔ `ResizeObserver` feedback loop that could thrash the terminal when Obsidian restores multiple sidebar panes on startup (#80).
- Cursor backend now calls the `cursor-agent` binary instead of `agent`, avoiding collisions with other CLIs on PATH.

### Changed
- Dropped unnecessary `!important` rules from `styles.css`.
- README + contributor docs cleanup (install path, backend list, release process).

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/derek-larson14/obsidian-claude-sidebar/compare/1.8.3...1.8.4

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description: Retagged from v1.8.3 (no v prefix) for Obsidian community plugin compatibility. No code changes.

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Windows: defensive fits for startup freeze (#69)

On Windows, Obsidian's layout dispatch occasionally crashes during startup (`recomputeChildrenDimensions` errors when iconize/BRAT load alongside the sidebar). When that happens, the terminal panel ends at the same dimensions it started — so neither `ResizeObserver` nor `workspace.onLayoutChange` fires, and xterm.js renders to a broken grid until the user manually resizes.

Adds fixed-interval defensive fits at 250ms, 750ms, 1.5s, and 3s after the terminal opens. Each fit is independent of any event listener and writes a `RESIZE` to the PTY — idempotent if dimensions are already correct.

Thanks to @MikiMorozov for the detailed console output that identified the layout crash as the root cause.

**Install:** Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your `.obsidian/plugins/claude-sidebar/` folder.
releases.name: v1.8.2

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Windows: drop idle CPU spin in terminal_win.py

The Windows PTY output reader was spinning on a non-blocking `pty.read()` with no backoff, pinning ~15% of one CPU core per idle wrapper. Now adds a 10ms sleep when `read()` returns empty, mirroring the `select(..., timeout=0.05)` pattern in the Unix path.

Reporter measured ~15% → 0.3–0.9% per-core idle CPU per wrapper.

Thanks to @Youhoney for the fix (#76).

**Install:** Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your `.obsidian/plugins/claude-sidebar/` folder.
releases.name: v1.8.1

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## New

- **Pi as a CLI backend** — pick it from the dropdown or the new switcher
- **Switch CLI provider… command** — quick searchable picker in the command palette to jump between backends without opening settings. Bind it to a hotkey (I'm using `Cmd+Shift+\`)
- **Per-provider CLI flags** — the "CLI flags" setting is now scoped to whichever backend is selected, so you can keep different `--model` flags for Claude vs Codex vs Gemini, etc. Existing flag value migrates to your current backend automatically on first load
- **GitHub Copilot CLI backend** — thanks @omarshahine (#70)
- **Better Windows pywinpty error message + portable build.sh** — thanks @omarshahine (#74). The error now prints the exact Python interpreter path so you know where to install pywinpty

## Thanks

@omarshahine for two solid PRs this cycle. 

## Install / Update

Manual: download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/claude-sidebar/` and reload.

BRAT users: auto-updates.
releases.name: v1.8.0

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description: Fix: Ctrl+O no longer breaks Obsidian's Quick Switcher when focus moves outside the sidebar. The binding is now registered only while the sidebar has focus.

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Fix #68: Ctrl+O now reliably expands command preview inside the sidebar terminal (switched to Obsidian's Scope API so we intercept before the global hotkey manager)
releases.name: v1.7.21

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Fix #68: Ctrl+O no longer triggers Obsidian's Quick Switcher when the terminal is focused — it's delivered to Claude Code for expanding truncated command previews
releases.name: v1.7.20

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Fix #67: force-kill PTY wrapper if SIGTERM stalls on tab close (prevents orphaned CLI processes from burning tokens)
releases.name: v1.7.19

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes

- Reverted theme-aware ANSI palette workaround — Claude Code v2.1.111+ now has native theme detection via OSC 11, so the plugin-side workaround is no longer needed.
- Restores `getThemeColors()` to its pre-workaround shape; keeps `COLORTERM=truecolor`.

## Thanks

Big thanks to @omarshahine for identifying that upstream Claude Code resolved this and submitting [#66](https://github.com/derek-larson14/obsidian-claude-sidebar/pull/66) to clean up the workaround.
releases.name: v1.7.18

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description: Fixes #65 — preserve terminal session across sidebar panel switches (no more repeated startup banners).
releases.name: v1.7.17

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Fix #64: call `super.setState()` in TerminalView for proper workspace history navigation (thanks @omarshahine)
releases.name: v1.7.16

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Fix #62: TerminalView.unload TypeError on workspace restore. Guard onClose with try-catch and prevent double-disposal during teardown.
releases.name: v1.7.15

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description: Increase PATH detection timeout from 2s to 5s for heavy shell configs (oh-my-zsh, powerlevel10k, conda). Add console warning on timeout.
releases.name: v1.7.14

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Prevent plugin from crashing Obsidian when terminal initialization fails (e.g. missing dependencies on Windows). Errors are now caught and logged to console instead of taking down the app.
releases.name: v1.7.13

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Fix #59: Workspace corruption when plugin enabled — view type now registered synchronously before async work, preventing race condition during workspace deserialization
releases.name: v1.7.12

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Fix Option+key for international keyboards in Electron.

PR #56's approach relied on xterm's keypress events, which don't fire reliably in Electron for Option+key. Now intercepts keydown and injects the character directly when it's a single printable character. Dead keys and Option+Arrow fall through to xterm as before.

Thanks to @orhankurulan for the fix (#57).

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
- Fix Option+key for international keyboard layouts on macOS (#56)
- Add Kimi Code as CLI backend option (#55)

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Fix Windows clipboard support:

- **Ctrl+V** now pastes from clipboard (Obsidian was intercepting the keydown before xterm.js could handle it)
- **Ctrl+C** copies selected text or sends interrupt (SIGINT) when nothing is selected
- **Right-click** pastes from clipboard (Windows only, matching Windows Terminal convention)

Mac behavior unchanged.

Fixes #53
releases.name: v1.7.9

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Increased scroll-lock grace period from 1s to 5s. When you scroll up to read history during active Claude output, the terminal no longer yanks you back to the bottom after just 1 second of idle time.

Fixes #52
releases.name: v1.7.8

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description: Fix scroll teleport bug (#51) — coordinated scroll management between stdout handler and fit() to eliminate race condition during Claude's TUI output.
releases.name: v1.7.7

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes

- Fix build.sh cross-platform compatibility — GNU sed on Linux/Windows now works correctly (#44, thanks @interatom!)
- Fix focus toggle skipping pinned leaves — toggling back to the editor now lands on your most recent non-pinned note instead of getting stuck on a pinned tab (#45, thanks @Spencerduran!)
- Fix activateView not focusing terminal — revealing an already-open terminal now properly receives keyboard input (#45)
- Remove experimental diff tracking feature (will return in a future release)

Thanks to @interatom and @Spencerduran for the contributions!
releases.name: v1.7.5

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
### Fix: Windows PTY input corruption from truncated escape sequences

Thanks to @interatom (#43) for diagnosing and fixing two bugs in the Windows terminal input loop:

- **Escape boundary corruption**: The 8-byte lookahead for `\x1b]RESIZE` could consume the leading `\x1b` of a subsequent escape sequence, causing the remainder to leak into cmd.exe as plain text
- **Terminal protocol response forwarding**: DA1/DA2 responses from xterm.js were passed through to cmd.exe, corrupting the command line

The fix replaces the fixed-size lookahead with proper escape sequence dispatch on the second byte (OSC vs CSI), and filters out terminal protocol responses that shouldn't reach the shell.
releases.name: v1.7.4

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Send selection context enrichment: file path, line numbers, and wikilink resolution added to selection context
releases.name: v1.7.3

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Fix Linux PermissionError when spawning terminal.

`pty.fork()` already isolates the child process via `setsid()` — the extra `os.setpgrp()` call was redundant and Linux rejects it. Removed.

Fixes #41 (thanks @Tsadoq for the diagnosis) and #40 (thanks @fyr-repo for reporting).

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
- Add right-click "Send selection to Claude" editor context menu
- Fix multi-tab targeting: send/toggle now targets last-focused Claude tab instead of always the first
releases.name: v1.7.1

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## What's new

**Multi-backend support** — Switch between coding agent CLIs in settings:
- Claude Code (default)
- Codex
- OpenCode
- Gemini CLI

YOLO mode and Resume commands auto-hide when the selected backend doesn't support them.

## Install / Update

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below into `.obsidian/plugins/claude-sidebar/`, then restart Obsidian.
releases.name: v1.7.0 — Multi-backend support

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Fix scroll stuck issue: fit() only scrolls to bottom if terminal was already at bottom
- Added stdout scroll snapping (near-bottom tolerance within 3 lines)
- Added "New Claude Tab (YOLO mode)" command palette entry
releases.name: v1.6.1

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## New features

- **Run Claude from this folder** — command palette + bot icon right-click. Starts Claude in the active file's directory. Falls back to vault root if no file is open.
- **Resume last conversation** — command palette + bot icon right-click. Uses `--continue` from your last working directory.

Both also available in the bot icon right-click menu alongside YOLO mode.

Closes #33, #34, #35
releases.name: v1.6.0

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes
- Fix #30: Chinese IME support on Windows (@Ephmeral) — CJK font fallbacks, UTF-8 multi-byte buffering, IME candidate window positioning
- Fix PTY signal handling race condition on macOS/Linux
releases.name: v1.5.23

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## What's New

- **YOLO mode**: Launch Claude with `--dangerously-skip-permissions` via right-click menus
  - Right-click the bot icon → "Open in YOLO mode"
  - Right-click any folder → "Open Claude here (YOLO)"

Thanks @dimpurr for the suggestion! (#27)
releases.name: v1.5.21

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## What's New

### New Commands (thanks @xaviuzz!)
- **Send File Path to Claude** - sends the absolute path of the active file to Claude terminal
- **Send Selection to Claude** - sends the currently selected text to Claude terminal

### Fixes
- Fixed light mode text selection visibility (thanks @peterkaminski!) - selection now uses a visible blue highlight in light mode
- Fixed timing issue when sending text to a terminal that isn't open yet - now properly waits for Claude to initialize

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Fix scroll jumping on message submit

The scroll position manager was too aggressive - it blocked any scroll with delta > 50 lines, which included legitimate scrolls when Claude responds. Now it only blocks jumps to the top (Ink TUI redraws).
releases.name: v1.5.18

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
### New Feature
- **Open Claude in subfolder**: Right-click any folder in Obsidian to open Claude with that folder as the working directory

Thanks to @heysarver for the contribution!
releases.name: v1.5.17: Folder context menu

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
### New Feature
- **Drag-and-drop file loading**: Drag files onto the Claude sidebar to insert their paths into the terminal
  - Works with files from Finder/external sources
  - Works with files from Obsidian's file explorer sidebar

Thanks to @OannesGPT for the suggestion!
releases.name: v1.5.16: Add drag-and-drop file loading

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Improve scroll position stability (#22)

Fix for terminal jumping to the top during long conversations.

### Changes
- `fit()` now preserves scroll position - saves position before resize and restores after via `requestAnimationFrame`
- Improved jump detection - catches any large sudden jump (>50 lines) instead of only jumps to position 0
releases.name: v1.5.15

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Fix: Terminal rows stuck at 24 on some vaults (#21)

Fixed a race condition where the terminal would start with only 24 rows regardless of window size. The issue occurred when `startShell()` read terminal dimensions before the font metrics were fully loaded.

### Changes
- Added `ensureFitWithRetry()` - retries fitting for up to 2 seconds until font metrics are ready
- Added post-shell-start resize verification at 500ms as a safety net

### Verification
Run `stty size` in the terminal - rows should now match your actual window height.

Thanks to @peterkaminski for reporting this issue!
releases.name: v1.5.14

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Fix PATH for Claude Code installation

Always add ~/.local/bin to PATH on non-Windows systems, ensuring Claude Code is found even when GUI apps don't inherit shell PATH configuration.
releases.name: v1.5.13

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Changes

- **Fix orphaned process cleanup**: Use process groups to kill entire process tree when terminal tab closes
  - Previously, closing tabs could leave dozens of orphaned Claude processes running
  - Now uses `os.setpgrp()` and `os.killpg()` to ensure all child processes are terminated
- Prevents memory/CPU buildup from accumulated orphaned processes

## Install

Download `main.js` and `manifest.json` and place them in your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/claude-sidebar/` folder.
releases.name: v1.5.12

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Fix orphaned shell processes

Terminal sessions now properly clean up child shell processes when closed, preventing orphaned processes from accumulating.
releases.name: v1.5.11

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description: Fix terminal jumping to top during Claude Code permission prompts

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- Fix Shift+Enter sometimes sending message instead of creating newline (#18)

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description: Auto-focus terminal input when opening new tab

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Add throttle to ribbon button to prevent accidental multi-open

1.5s debounce prevents opening multiple tabs from rapid clicks while Claude is starting up.

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
Add image paste support

Pasting images now saves them to a temp file and inserts the path into the terminal. Claude CLI natively supports image files by path.

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Improvements

- **Ribbon button now opens a new tab** instead of focusing existing
  - Makes multiple Claude instances more discoverable
  - No need to use Command Palette for multi-Claude workflows
releases.name: v1.5.5: Easier multi-Claude access

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- **Improved Windows Python detection for pyenv-win users**
  - Added support for `.bat` shims used by pyenv-win
  - Fixed line ending handling in `where.exe` output
  - Python detection now tries: py launcher → pyenv-win .bat shims → first valid path → fallback to `python`
releases.name: v1.5.4: Improved Windows Python detection

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- **Fix PATH inheritance for GUI-launched Obsidian on macOS/Linux** ([#6](https://github.com/derek-larson14/obsidian-claude-sidebar/pull/6))
  - Tools like `claude`, `bun`, and nvm-managed `node` now work correctly when Obsidian is launched from Finder/Dock
  - Thanks to [@hlibkoval](https://github.com/hlibkoval) for the contribution!

- Reduced PATH capture timeout from 5s to 2s for snappier terminal startup
releases.name: v1.5.3: Fix PATH for GUI-launched Obsidian

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## What's New

- **Cleaner startup**: Claude launches directly without showing the shell prompt first
- **Faster startup**: Removed 300ms delay on Mac/Linux
- **Shell fallback**: Ctrl+C twice from Claude drops you into a shell (type `claude` to restart)
- **Simplified internals**: Removed internal xterm API calls that could cause issues

## Notes
- Windows unchanged (still uses the auto-type approach)
- Works with bash, zsh, and most POSIX shells
releases.name: v1.5.2: Direct Claude launch for cleaner startup UX

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Fixed sidebar freezing** - Removed scroll state tracking that caused scroll→fit→scroll loops
- **Fixed theme observer** - Only updates theme when colors actually change

## Changes

- Simplified scroll handling by trusting xterm.js native behavior
- Increased fit debounce from 50ms to 100ms
- Removed ~77 lines of unnecessary complexity
releases.name: v1.5.1

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## What's New

- **Windows support (experimental)** - Claude Sidebar now works on Windows via pywinpty
- Windows-friendly fonts (Cascadia Mono, Consolas)
- Filter focus/resize escape sequences for cleaner terminal output

## Windows Requirements

1. Python 3 from [python.org](https://python.org)
2. pywinpty: `pip install pywinpty`

See [Windows Setup](https://github.com/derek-larson14/obsidian-claude-sidebar#windows-setup-experimental) for full instructions.

## Installation

**Mac/Linux:**
\`\`\`bash
curl -sL https://github.com/derek-larson14/obsidian-claude-sidebar/archive/refs/heads/main.tar.gz | tar -xz -C .obsidian/plugins && mv .obsidian/plugins/obsidian-claude-sidebar-main .obsidian/plugins/claude-sidebar
\`\`\`

**Windows:** See README for PowerShell instructions.
releases.name: v1.5.0 - Windows Support (Experimental)

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description: - Bump version to 1.4.2\n- Remove custom key scroll guard to avoid menu toggle issues
releases.name: v1.4.2

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Bug Fix

**Fixed PTY zombie process spin loop** - When Obsidian disconnects (plugin reload, app restart), PTY processes would previously spin at 100% CPU indefinitely. Now they exit cleanly.

### Files
- `main.js` - Plugin code
- `manifest.json` - Plugin manifest
- `styles.css` - Plugin styles
releases.name: v1.4.1

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## What's Changed
* Feature/scroll and utf8 fixes by @derek-larson14 in https://github.com/derek-larson14/obsidian-claude-sidebar/pull/1

## New Contributors
* @derek-larson14 made their first contribution in https://github.com/derek-larson14/obsidian-claude-sidebar/pull/1

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/derek-larson14/obsidian-claude-sidebar/compare/v1.3.1...v1.4.0
releases.name: v1.4.0

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## Bug Fix

**Critical fix for runaway CPU usage**

When terminal sessions ended (shell exited or was killed), the PTY wrapper process would spin at 100% CPU indefinitely instead of exiting cleanly. This could result in multiple zombie processes consuming all CPU resources.

### If you're affected
If your Mac has been running hot or slow, check for zombie processes:
```bash
ps aux | grep terminal_pty
```

Kill any you find:
```bash
pkill -f "terminal_pty.py"
```

Then update to this version.
releases.name: v1.3.1 - Fix CPU spin bug

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
## What's Changed

- **Simplified installation**: PTY script is now embedded in main.js as base64, eliminating the need for a separate terminal_pty.py file
- **Automatic updates**: PTY script is always written fresh to /tmp on terminal spawn, ensuring updates take effect immediately
- Installation now requires only 3 files: `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`

## Installation

### Via BRAT
Add `derek-larson14/obsidian-claude-sidebar` in BRAT settings.

### Manual
Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below and place in `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/claude-sidebar/`
releases.name: v1.3.0

releases.author: derek-larson14
releases.description:
- Fix installation docs, add curl install option
- Add Cmd+Arrow support for cursor navigation
- Use manifest.id for plugin path instead of hardcoding
- Fix text rendering and background color issues
- Fix escape key handling, add toggle focus command
